title: QUIC Interop Transfer Completion Work Item
completion_notes: Optional implementation notes, deviations, or follow-up items.
out_of_scope: Retry enablement in the QUIC interop runner.
Broader 1-RTT data-path claims, trust-store policy widening, hostname validation, certificate-path validation, revocation, 0-RTT, or key update.
summary: Define and, when implementation follows, land the narrow child-process transfer contract on the existing active-phase managed path without widening the public or unsupported boundary.
verification_plan: Run the existing handshake/public guards and CRT readiness proofs, add a focused transfer requirement-home test that exercises one bidirectional stream on the child-process harness path, and confirm the harness only reports exit 0 after byte delivery and EOF on both sides. Keep `transfer` and `retry` returning `127` until that proof exists.
planned_changes:
- Add a transfer testcase dispatch branch in `InteropHarnessRunner` only when the contract is implemented.
- Use the existing active-phase managed path to open or accept one application stream and gate exit 0 on byte delivery plus EOF.
- Preserve the fixed `/www` and `/downloads` mount-path contract without widening retry, multi-stream behavior, or TLS policy.
- Add requirement-home coverage for the new transfer contract and keep the unsupported-testcase guards honest.
